rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Respondent Co-Trustees "Clawback" Motion To Compel Return Of Privileged Document
rulings.Rulings: The motion is granted. The March 19, 2015 letter agreement set out how the parties would treat the dispute about the applicability of attorney-client privilege to the presentation document "[r]ather than litigate this issue." Petioner, however, chose to litigate the issue, and the Court has determined that the presentation document is privileged. It follows from that determination that the document must be treated as privileged as of the date of that ruling. Petitioner and his attorneys must return or certify the destruction of all copies of the September 26, 2012 presentation document other than any copies that are attached as exhibits to pleadings not filed under seal. Petitioner and his attorneys, going forward, must proceed as if they had not received and did not have access to the contents of the presentation document. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise opposing counsel and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, October 18, 2016.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Appointing Discovery Referee
rulings.Rulings: The motion to appoint a discovery referee is granted. All parties agree that a referee is warranted in light of the number and scope of pending discovery motions on file and anticipated. The principal dispute goes to whether petitioner should be compelled to bear his share of costs. Petitioner has failed to establish an economic inablility to bear his pro rata share of costs. This ruling and the apportionment ruling in the next sentence are without prejudice to any party requesting a modification on the basis of a recommendation by the referee in accordance with CRC 3.922(f)(3). Absent an agreement of the parties, each party making a motion, opposing a motion, or joining in an opposition or motion shall be apportioned one share of the referee's fees as to that motion. The referee's fees shall not exceed $120,000 absent agreement of the parties or further order of the Court. All motions currently noticed for November 16, 2016 or already allowed to be noticed for November 17, 2016 are referred to the referee. This reference will include any other discovery matters as to which the parties agree to a referral. Prior to filing any further discovery motions, the parties must meet and confer concerning whether to refer such motions as well and provide a certificate of having done so. The maximum hourly rate will be specified in the order identifying the referee. The parties may agree on a referee on or before October 27, 2016. If they fail to agree, respondents (as a group) and petitioner may each nominate up to three individuals by October 27, 2016. As to each individual nominated, the nominating party must submit the nominee's professional credentials, billing rate, and the nominee's certificate pursuant to CRC 3.924(a)(1) along with a proposed order specifying appointment of that individual. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so advise opposing parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, October 18, 2016.
